name: Risotto Con Calamari (Rice with Squid)
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Fish broth or clam juice (heated to a boil)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the squid, reserving their ink sacs and tentacles. Cut the squid into 1/2-inch-wide rings or pieces. Chop the tentacles.
2. In a wide, shallow casserole, preferably earthenware and about 12 inches across, heat the olive oil and sauté the onion until it is wilted. Add the squid rings and tentacles and sauté for 5 minutes. Then add the chopped pimiento, tomato, garlic, parsley, salt, pepper, saffron, and chili pepper. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
3. Break the ink sacs into a cup and mix with the wine. Pass this mixture through a sieve several times until most of the ink is extracted. Reserve.
4. Add the rice and boiling hot broth to the casserole and stir in the ink mixture. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a boil and cook over medium-high heat, uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes, or until the rice is no longer soupy but some liquid remains.
5. Decorate with the pimiento strips and transfer to an oven preheated to 325 degrees. Bake for 15 minutes, uncovered, until the liquid is absorbed but the rice is not quite done. Remove from the oven and cover lightly with foil; let sit for 10 minutes.
6. While the rice is resting, make the garlic sauce. Place the crushed garlic in a processor or blender. Very gradually, with the motor running, pour in the oil. Blend until smooth. Serve separately.
This recipe is based on Penelope Casas' Arroz Negro from her excellent book The Foods and Wines of Spain. The result is a superb, rich black risotto. Use only Italian short-grain rice; otherwise, the dish will not work. Yield: 4-6 servings. Note: Serve this dish with a salad and a dry white wine.
